2604.15190.title: Meituan Merchant Business Diagnosis via Policy-Guided Dual-Process User Simulation
2604.15190.paper_summary: Simulating group-level user behavior enables scalable counterfactual evaluation of merchant strategies without costly online experiments. However, building a trustworthy simulator faces two structural challenges. First, information incompleteness causes reasoning-based simulators to over-rationalize when unobserved factors such as offline context and implicit habits are missing. Second, mechanism duality requires capturing both interpretable preferences and implicit statistical regularities, which no single paradigm achieves alone. We propose Policy-Guided Hybrid Simulation (PGHS), a dual-process framework that mines transferable decision policies from behavioral trajectories and uses them as a shared alignment layer. This layer anchors an LLM-based reasoning branch that prevents over-rationalization and an ML-based fitting branch that absorbs implicit regularities. Group-level predictions from both branches are fused for complementary correction. We deploy PGHS on Meituan with 101 merchants and over 26,000 trajectories. PGHS achieves a group simulation error of 8.80%, improving over the best reasoning-based and fitting-based baselines by 45.8% and 40.9% respectively.
2604.15190.paper_summary_zh: 模擬群體層級的用戶行為使得商家策略的可擴展反事實評估成為可能，而無需昂貴的在線實驗。<lb/>然而，建立一個可信的模擬器面臨兩個結構性挑戰。<lb/>首先，信息不完整導致基於推理的模擬器在缺乏未觀察因素（如線下環境和隱性習慣）時過度理性化。<lb/>其次，機制的雙重性要求同時捕捉可解釋的偏好和隱性統計規律，這是任何單一範式無法單獨實現的。<lb/>我們提出了政策引導的混合模擬（PGHS），這是一個雙過程框架，從行為軌跡中挖掘可轉移的決策政策，並將其用作共享對齊層。<lb/>這一層固定了一個基於LLM的推理分支，防止過度理性化，以及一個基於ML的擬合分支，吸收隱性規律。<lb/>來自兩個分支的群體層級預測被融合以進行互補修正。<lb/>我們在美團上部署PGHS，涵蓋101家商家和超過26,000條軌跡。<lb/>PGHS達到了8.80%的群體模擬誤差，分別比最佳的基於推理和基於擬合的基準提高了45.8%和40.9%。
2604.15190.author: Ziyang Chen et.al.
2604.15190.authors: Ziyang Chen, Renbing Chen, Daowei Li, Jinzhi Liao, Jiashen Sun, Ke Zeng, Xiang Zhao
